content.problemStatement:
A positive integer N≥1N\ge 1N≥1 is given.
An NNN-uple (x1,x2,…,xN)(x_1, x_2, \dots, x_N)(x1​,x2​,…,xN​) of nonnegative integers is special if, after we remove all entries which are zero, its entries are nondecreasing. For example (0,2,0,0,4,0,4,5,0,0)(0, 2, 0, 0, 4, 0, 4, 5, 0, 0)(0,2,0,0,4,0,4,5,0,0) is special, but (3,2,0)(3, 2, 0)(3,2,0) and (0,1,3,0,0,1,0)(0, 1, 3, 0, 0, 1, 0)(0,1,3,0,0,1,0) are not.

One can show that there is a unique function μ\muμ defined on the NNN-uples of nonnegative integers such that, for any NNN-uple x=(x1,x2,…,xN)x=(x_1, x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1​,x2​,…,xN​)

∑y≤xμ(y)={1if x is special0otherwise,\sum_{y \le x} \mu(y) =
\begin{cases}
    1 &\text{if $x$ is special} \\
    0 &\text{otherwise}
\end{cases},y≤x∑​μ(y)={10​if x is specialotherwise​,where the sum ranges over all NNN-uples y=(y1,y2,…,yN)y=(y_1, y_2,\dots, y_N)y=(y1​,y2​,…,yN​) of nonnegative integers such that yi≤xiy_i\le x_iyi​≤xi​ for all 1≤i≤N1\le i\le N1≤i≤N.

You are given N,M≥1N, M \ge 1N,M≥1. Compute the remainder modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353 of the number of NNN-uples x=(x1,x2,…,xN)x=(x_1,x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1​,x2​,…,xN​) such that 0≤xi≤M0\le x_i\le M0≤xi​≤M for all 1≤i≤N1\le i\le N1≤i≤N and μ(x)=0\mu(x) = 0μ(x)=0.


Input Format

The only line contains two positive integers NNN, MMM, the size of the tuples and the maximum possible value of the entries of the tuples.


Output Format
Print the the number of NNN-uples x=(x1,x2,…,xN)x=(x_1,x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1​,x2​,…,xN​) such that 0≤xi≤M0\le x_i\le M0≤xi​≤M for all 1≤i≤N1\le i\le N1≤i≤N and μ(x)=0\mu(x) = 0μ(x)=0. As it can be large, print it modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For N=1N=1N=1, one can prove that μ((0))=1\mu((0))=1μ((0))=1 and μ((k))=0\mu((k))=0μ((k))=0 for all k≥1k\ge 1k≥1. Hence, since M=3M=3M=3, the 111-uples xxx such that μ(x)=0\mu(x)=0μ(x)=0 are (1)(1)(1), (2)(2)(2) and (3)(3)(3). Therefore the answer to the first sample is 333.
